Title: Validation of an updated Associative Transcriptomics platform for the polyploid crop species Brassica napus: dissection of the genetic architecture of erucic acid and tocopherol isoform variation in seeds

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: RIPR diversity panel (383 doubled haploid/inbred accessions)
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Very-long-chain fatty acid biosynthesis / erucic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Major locus for seed erucic acid content; useful for marker-assisted breeding to select either low-erucic edible oil types or high-erucic industrial oil types. Should be considered together with the homoeologous C3 FAE1 locus because both main loci strongly affect the trait. In this paper the evidence is association-based rather than transgenic editing in B. napus.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the condensation step of fatty acid elongation, extending oleoyl-derived acyl-CoA into very-long-chain fatty acids that lead to erucic acid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with increased seed erucic acid content; this A8-region FAE1 orthologue lies near the center of one of the two strongest association peaks controlling the trait.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Associative Transcriptomics/GWAS using transcriptome SNPs and GEMs across 376 accessions; positional colocalization of the gene model with the major A8 association peak for seed erucic acid.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly contributes to synthesis of the target very-long-chain fatty acid product.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seeds produced from plants grown in randomized blocks under controlled polytunnel conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: The paper states the main FAE1 control genes are transcriptionally inactive in the leaf tissue used to generate functional genotypes; seed expression was not measured here.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: The paper states the main FAE1 control genes are transcriptionally inactive in the leaf tissue used to generate functional genotypes; seed expression was not measured here.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed elongation step in the very-long-chain fatty acid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: mRNA-seq/RNA-seq for SNP discovery and expression quantification; SRA: PRJNA309367
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as one of the known major control genes for seed erucic acid. Strong signals were detected even though the gene itself was not expressed in sampled leaves, because linked markers in LD captured the locus.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Erucic acid branch of very-long-chain monounsaturated fatty acid elongation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Selection for low erucic acid in canola breeding has created strong LD and co-selection with glucosinolate loci, which can reduce mapping resolution. Altering this locus changes oil end-use quality (edible versus industrial).
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Elongated very-long-chain acyl-CoA intermediates leading to erucic acid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established major control locus for seed erucic acid in this study
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cab035983.1, a Brassica napus orthologue of Arabidopsis FAE1, colocalized with a major association peak on chromosome A8 for seed erucic acid content. 2. The A8 FAE1 locus was one of the two principal loci explaining variation in seed erucic acid across the RIPR panel. 3. Although FAE1 transcripts were not detected in leaves used for functional genotyping, LD with nearby markers still enabled detection of the locus by Associative Transcriptomics.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines rapeseed oil suitability for edible versus industrial uses.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: RIPR diversity panel (383 doubled haploid/inbred accessions)
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Very-long-chain fatty acid biosynthesis / erucic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Major locus for marker-assisted selection of seed erucic acid phenotype. Should be deployed jointly with the A8 FAE1 locus for robust breeding of low-erucic or high-erucic germplasm. Evidence here is positional and association-based.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a fatty acid elongation condensation step that supports biosynthesis of very-long-chain monounsaturated fatty acids, including erucic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with increased seed erucic acid content; this C3-region FAE1 orthologue lies near the center of the second strongest major association peak.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Associative Transcriptomics/GWAS using transcriptome SNPs and GEMs across 376 accessions; positional colocalization with the major C3 association peak for seed erucic acid.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly contributes to synthesis of the target very-long-chain fatty acid product.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seeds produced from plants grown in randomized blocks under controlled polytunnel conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: The paper states the main FAE1 control genes are transcriptionally inactive in the leaf tissue used to generate functional genotypes; seed expression was not measured here.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: The paper states the main FAE1 control genes are transcriptionally inactive in the leaf tissue used to generate functional genotypes; seed expression was not measured here.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed elongation step in the very-long-chain fatty acid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: mRNA-seq/RNA-seq for SNP discovery and expression quantification; SRA: PRJNA309367
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as the homoeologous major erucic-acid control locus on chromosome C3. The study treats the A8 and C3 FAE1 orthologues as the two main loci controlling seed erucic acid content.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Erucic acid branch of very-long-chain monounsaturated fatty acid elongation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Same breeding tradeoff as the A8 locus: lowering erucic acid improves edible quality but reduces industrial-oil trait value; breeding history and LD may confound nearby associations.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Elongated very-long-chain acyl-CoA intermediates leading to erucic acid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established major control locus for seed erucic acid in this study
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bo3g168810.1, a Brassica napus orthologue of Arabidopsis FAE1, colocalized with the major chromosome C3 association peak for seed erucic acid. 2. Together with the A8 FAE1 orthologue, it represents one of the two principal loci controlling erucic acid variation in the panel. 3. The locus was detectable by association mapping despite lack of FAE1 expression in the sampled leaf tissue, owing to linked markers.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines rapeseed oil suitability for edible versus industrial uses.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: RIPR diversity panel (377 doubled haploid/inbred accessions)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate gene and predictive marker source for improving seed tocopherol isoform composition, especially increasing α-tocopherol proportion. The tightly linked SNPs and the gene-expression marker showed good predictive ability and are promising for marker-assisted breeding. Upregulating favorable VTE4 expression is expected to shift γ/α ratio toward more α-tocopherol.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates γ-tocopherol to produce α-tocopherol, and also uses δ-tocopherol to produce β-tocopherol.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression is associated with lower seed γ/α-tocopherol ratio, consistent with increased conversion of γ-tocopherol into α-tocopherol.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Associative Transcriptomics/GWAS for SNP and GEM markers across 377 accessions; the gene was the most highly associated GEM on C2; expression-trait regression in leaves versus seed γ/α ratio; marker prediction by take-one-out permutation.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the target tocopherol isoform α-tocopherol from γ-tocopherol.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seeds produced from plants grown in randomized blocks under controlled polytunnel conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression in leaves was measurable and negatively correlated with seed γ/α-tocopherol ratio; tocopherols are noted to accumulate in all tissues, generally highest in seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression in leaves was measurable and negatively correlated with seed γ/α-tocopherol ratio; tocopherols are noted to accumulate in all tissues, generally highest in seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: mRNA-seq/RNA-seq for SNP discovery and expression quantification; SRA: PRJNA309367
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located in the sharp major association peak on chromosome C2. No significant SNP within the gene itself was associated with the trait, suggesting the causal variation is primarily expression variation rather than coding-sequence polymorphism. A homoeologous VTE4-containing region on A2 also showed weaker association.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Tocopherol methylation branch controlling γ- versus α-tocopherol composition
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear growth penalty was reported. Altering VTE4 mainly changes tocopherol isoform balance rather than total tocopherol per se; broader effects on oxidation protection were not directly tested in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal determinant of tocopherol isoform composition in this study
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bo2g050970.1, a Brassica napus orthologue of Arabidopsis VTE4, was identified within the strongest chromosome C2 association peak for seed γ/α-tocopherol ratio. 2. It was the most highly associated gene-expression marker on C2, and its leaf expression was negatively correlated with the seed γ/α ratio, matching its predicted γ-TMT activity. 3. The associated marker set showed strong predictive power for tocopherol composition, supporting utility in marker-assisted breeding.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant that protects seed oil against oxidation and contributes vitamin E nutritional quality.
